mnemonics: TCP: The Careful Postman (ensures reliable, ordered delivery)
Remember the 3-Way Handshake: SYN, SYN-ACK, ACK (Synchronize, Acknowledge Synchronization, Acknowledge)
keyPoints: Connection-Oriented: Requires a handshake to establish a connection before data transfer.
Reliable Delivery: Uses sequence numbers, acknowledgements, and retransmissions to guarantee data arrives.
Flow Control: Uses a sliding window mechanism to prevent a fast sender from overwhelming a slow receiver.
Congestion Control: Dynamically adjusts transmission rate to avoid network congestion (e.g., slow start, congestion avoidance).
commonMistakes: Confusing TCP with UDP: TCP is connection-oriented and reliable; UDP is connectionless and unreliable.
Mixing up SEQ and ACK numbers: The ACK number is the next SEQ number the receiver expects, acknowledging all prior data.
Forgetting about the established state: Assuming the connection is ready after the initial SYN and SYN-ACK; the final ACK is crucial.
